title: Today's AI Brief: The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
summary: A look at the Vatican's involvement in AI and recent announcements from Google I/O 2026.
imageAlt: Image from source article for The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's AI Brief. I've been digging into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence, and I'm excited to share them with you. It's been a busy week, with news from the Vatican and Google's I/O conference. Let's dive in.

First up, the Vatican has a man inside Anthropic, a leading AI research organization. Chris Olah, an atheist and former Google researcher, has been making waves with his unique perspective on AI safety. He recently spoke at a ceremony following Pope Leo's historic AI encyclical, where the pontiff called for 'disarming' AI technology. What's fascinating here is that Olah's views on AI safety align with the Vatican's concerns, despite their differing backgrounds. It's not every day you see an atheist and the Vatican on the same page, but it just goes to show that AI safety is a universal concern.

Next, Google's I/O 2026 conference was a whirlwind of announcements, with 100 new developments in AI, technology, and more. I'm not going to try to cover all 100, but a few items caught my eye. The company is clearly pushing the limits of what's possible with AI, from advancing model economics to expanding data boundaries. I'm curious to see how these innovations play out and what they mean for the future of AI.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
